highlights.title: Samsung-backed AI chip firm Rebellions targets IPO in South Korea next year, CEO tells CNBC
highlights.description: Sunghyun Park, CEO of Rebellions, told CNBC that the company is leaning toward a listing on the KOSPI over the KOSDAQ.

highlights.title: China warns about AI risks with Anthropic's Claude Code
highlights.description: China said specific versions of Claude Code posed back-door vulnerabilities that could send sensitive information to a remote server.

highlights.title: Samsung will launch its new wide foldable on July 22nd
highlights.description:
Samsung has announced that its next Galaxy Unpacked launch event will be held on July 22nd, with the tagline: "A new shape unfolds."
It's long been rumored that Samsung is about to expand its foldable phone line to a third format, with a shorter and wider version of its big book-style foldables, to match Huawei's Pura X Max and Apple's expected foldable iPhone. Today's announcement adds to that impression, showing a tall ticket with the stub torn off, leaving it shorter than before.
Alongside the wider foldable, Samsung is expected to announce updated versions of its existing Flip and Fold phones, with the latter potentially rebranded to t …
Read the full story at The Verge.

highlights.title: Apple Loses Spat With EU Over App Store and iPhone Rules
highlights.description: Apple Inc. lost a European Union court fight to stop its App Store and iPhone operating system being hit by the full-force of the bloc’s antitrust crackdown on Big Tech.

highlights.title: Free Waymo Rides in California? You Can Thank a Regulatory Quirk
highlights.description: A key delay from a state agency means robotaxi rides in the company’s new Ojai vehicle might be free for a few more months.

highlights.title: SambaNova hits $11 billion valuation as investors back Nvidia chip challengers
highlights.description: SambaNova is valued at $11 billion after fresh financing led by General Atlantic, as AI chip startups look to challenge Nvidia.

highlights.title: This former Apple executive is betting on Shenzhen, not Silicon Valley, to create the 'next Apple'
highlights.description: Startups building consumer electronics will have a better shot at becoming the next Apple in Shenzhen than in Silicon Valley, said Will Wang, CEO of China's smart-glasses startup.

highlights.title: OpenAI to Roll Out Top AI Model Globally After Limited Preview
highlights.description: OpenAI is set to release its most advanced model widely on Thursday and is expanding its preview globally, the company said.

highlights.title: AI Chip Startup SambaNova Raises Funds at $11 Billion Valuation
highlights.description: Chip startup SambaNova Systems Inc. has raised $1 billion at an $11 billion valuation, underscoring investors’ conviction in rising demand for infrastructure tied to artificial intelligence.

highlights.title: AI chip maker SambaNova raises $1B at $11B valuation, 5 months after last mega round
highlights.description: AI chip maker SambaNova has raised at an $11B valuation months after Intel was rumored to be trying to buy it for about $1.6 billion.

business.title: KKR-Backed SmartHR Is Said to Delay Tokyo IPO Beyond This Year
business.description: Japanese human resources platform operator SmartHR Inc. has delayed its plans for an initial public offering to next year at the earliest as investors viewed the company’s targeted valuation as too high, according to people familiar with the matter.

ai.title: Temasek Assets Break $400 Billion as AI Bets Boost Returns
ai.description: Singaporean state-owned investor Temasek Holdings Pte is ramping up its exposure to artificial intelligence and the Americas, a shift which helped push its net portfolio value to S$518 billion ($401 billion) in the last financial year.

ai.title: Australian Pension Shrugs Off AI Bubble Fears, Plans to Buy Dips
ai.description: UniSuper, one of Australia’s largest pension funds, is looking to buy any pullback in US technology stocks, shrugging off concerns over lofty valuations as it bets artificial intelligence will fuel years of earnings growth.

ai.title: NHS told to show its working on Palantir platform benefits
ai.description: Campaigners say ministers repeated claims about FDP despite an admission that the data does not prove cause and effect

ai.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
ai.description: ZML, a hot French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has now released ZML/LLMD, software that could make running AI less costly.

ev_autonomous.title: Apple begins testing CXMT chips for devices sold in China, FT says
Apple Loses EU Fight Over App Store Gatekeeper Label
ev_autonomous.description:
Apple's challenge against the EU's designation of its App Stores and iOS platform as "gatekeepers" was dismissed by Europe's top court on Wednesday, reports Reuters.

"The General Court dismisses Apple's actions regarding its designation as a gatekeeper in relation to the App Store and iOS," the Luxembourg-based tribunal said.
Apple took its case to Luxembourg's General Court in 2024 after the European Commission designated its five App Stores – on the iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple TV, and Apple Watch – as a single core platform service under the Digital Markets Act (DMA), a label that brings with it a set of strict obligations.

Designated "gatekeepers" are prohibited from favoring their own services over those of rivals, and are prevented from combining personal data across different services. They also have to give users the option to use alternative app stores.

Apple also challenged the EU's designation of iOS as a gateway platform, a status that requires the operating system allows rival services to interoperate with it.

The company also disputed the classification of iMessage as a number-independent interpersonal communications service, or NIICS, which would subject the app to EU telecoms rules. But the General Court said Apple's actions regarding the iMessage service are inadmissible.

Tags: Apple Antitrust, App Store, European Commission, European Union
This article, "Apple Loses EU Fight Over App Store Gatekeeper Label" first appeared on MacRumors.com

regulation.title: Meta says it will disable the camera on its glasses if you tamper with the recording LED
regulation.description: Meta answers people's privacy concerns about its smart glasses in an FAQ.

chips_cloud.title: Goldman Sachs Says Hyperscalers Are 'Well-Positioned'
chips_cloud.description: Christian Mueller-Glissmann, managing director for portfolio strategy and asset allocation at Goldman Sachs, says the levered positioning toward semiconductors got a bit "extreme" and that the trend is reversing. "The hyperscalers are quite well-positioned right now because they still own a lot of the infrastructure," Mueller-Glissmann tells Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)
